codeReview.summary: Commit `d38896db0846c26d5b39b34c39faf75ed3885a67` does restore HEAD's version metadata to `0.4.60`, and the separate AI SDK compatibility blocker from the prior `extension-refactor-ai-sdk` review appears addressed by `ef83ed72ba2d90b4a0b3221327191aebbd117bab` plus the current alias-aware `message-factory.ts`/`message-types.ts`. But this fix feature still fails its own contract: it did not create clean replacement commits for the five failed refactors. The original commits `b2af1aaf5d8acde300b1b777930048db957615a0`, `80a94959b2c822cd9cecb3360c65e50d10350fd5`, `a1c32b9bd31f6c722e58a02aa6bc96430e369731`, `0f8b3ee4480885306977832351478b6313d2c387`, and `b2ce138f06ce8647606cdb940fd1963e01b9e0bd` still remain in git history with bundled edits to the versioned metadata files, so the per-feature scope violations from those prior reviews are not actually cleared.

issues.description: The rerun does not create the requested clean replacement commits for agent-loop, tool-executor, error-handling, AI SDK, and tools refactors. Instead, commit `d38896db0846c26d5b39b34c39faf75ed3885a67` only reverts `package.json:3` from `0.4.67` back to `0.4.60` after the fact, while the original reviewed commits `b2af1aa`, `80a9495`, `a1c32b9`, `0f8b3ee`, and `b2ce138` still each contain their own out-of-scope root version bump in history. That does not satisfy the task requirement to create clean refactor commits without package or manifest changes.

The same unresolved history problem remains in `packages/extension/manifest.json:4`: each original failed refactor commit still bumps the Chrome manifest version inside its own diff, and `d38896...` only flips the line back later. Because no replacement refactor commits were created, the version-bump blocker for all five original features still stands.

The Firefox manifest churn was likewise only net-reverted in `d38896...`; the original commits for `extension-refactor-agent-loop`, `extension-refactor-tool-executor`, `extension-refactor-error-handling`, `extension-refactor-ai-sdk`, and `extension-refactor-tools` still each include `packages/extension/manifest.firefox.json:15` in their own commit diffs. The fix therefore restores the baseline at HEAD but does not clear the original per-feature scrutiny failures.

addressesFailureFrom: Multiple prior reviews: extension-refactor-agent-loop -> /Users/sero/projects/browser-ai/.factory/validation/extension-core/scrutiny/reviews/extension-refactor-agent-loop.json; extension-refactor-tool-executor -> /Users/sero/projects/browser-ai/.factory/validation/extension-core/scrutiny/reviews/extension-refactor-tool-executor.json; extension-refactor-error-handling -> /Users/sero/projects/browser-ai/.factory/validation/extension-core/scrutiny/reviews/extension-refactor-error-handling.json; extension-refactor-ai-sdk -> /Users/sero/projects/browser-ai/.factory/validation/extension-core/scrutiny/reviews/extension-refactor-ai-sdk.json; extension-refactor-tools -> /Users/sero/projects/browser-ai/.factory/validation/extension-core/scrutiny/reviews/extension-refactor-tools.json
summary: Fail. I reviewed the fix handoff/transcript, commit `d38896`, the five original failed commits, and the later `ef83ed7` AI compatibility fix. HEAD is back at `0.4.60` and I did not find another surviving blocker from the earlier AI SDK review, but the requested clean replacement refactor commits were never created, so the original version-bump scope violations remain unresolved in git history.
